story.iWant: per-tenant resource quotas (event rate, storage, query limits)
story.soThat: one tenant cannot impact system performance for others
story.tasks: - AC1: TenantQuotaManager.kt implements quota enforcement - AC2: Quotas configurable per tenant: max_events_per_second, max_storage_mb, max_query_complexity - AC3: Rate limiting using token bucket algorithm (Bucket4j or similar) - AC4: Quota exceeded returns 429 Too Many Requests with retry-after header - AC5: Quota usage tracked in Prometheus metrics per tenant - AC6: Hot tenant detection (sustained quota violations) - AC7: Integration test validates quota enforcement

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. TenantQuotaManager.kt implements quota enforcement 2. Quotas configurable per tenant: max_events_per_second, max_storage_mb, max_query_complexity 3. Rate limiting using token bucket algorithm (Bucket4j or similar) 4. Quota exceeded returns 429 Too Many Requests with retry-after header 5. Quota usage tracked in Prometheus metrics per tenant 6. Hot tenant detection (sustained quota violations) 7. Integration test validates quota enforcement

doc.relevance: Resource quota enforcement prevents noisy neighbor problems in multi-tenant systems
doc.snippet: Per-Tenant Resource Quotas: - Quotas: max_events_per_second (rate limiting), max_storage_mb (storage limits), max_query_complexity (query cost) - Token Bucket Algorithm: Fair rate limiting with burst capacity (Bucket4j library) - Quota Exceeded: 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header (RFC 6585) - Hot Tenant Detection: Sustained quota violations (e.g., >80% quota usage for >5 minutes) - Prometheus Metrics: Quota usage per tenant (current usage, limit, percentage) - Configuration: Per-tenant quotas in database or configuration service

doc.relevance: Business requirements for resource quota management
doc.snippet: FR004 includes per-tenant resource quotas to prevent noisy neighbor problems. Fair resource allocation ensures one tenant cannot degrade service for others. Quotas enforce SLA boundaries and enable tiered pricing models.

doc.relevance: PR #92 identified need for circuit breakers and bulkheads for resource isolation
doc.snippet: PR #92 OWASP Gap: A10 Exception Handling - 60/100 - No circuit breakers (Resilience4j) - No event processor retry strategies - No bulkheads for resource isolation Risk for Epic 4: One tenant's errors impact all tenants (cascading failures) Solution: Resilience4j for circuit breakers, rate limiters, bulkheads

Story 4.9: Per-Tenant Resource Quotas - TenantQuotaManager.kt implements quota enforcement - Quotas: max_events_per_second, max_storage_mb, max_query_complexity - Rate limiting using token bucket algorithm (Bucket4j) - Quota exceeded returns 429 Too Many Requests with retry-after header - Hot tenant detection (sustained quota violations)

artifact.reason: Story 4.8 metrics patterns for quota usage tracking
Quota enforcement via Axon interceptor (command rate limiting)

constraints.constraint: Three quota types enforced per tenant: 1. max_events_per_second: Command rate limiting (prevents event storm) 2. max_storage_mb: Aggregate storage limits (prevents unbounded growth) 3. max_query_complexity: Query cost limits (prevents expensive queries) Configurable per tenant via database or configuration service.

Token Bucket Algorithm for rate limiting (Bucket4j library): - Refill rate: max_events_per_second tokens/second - Burst capacity: 2x refill rate (allows short bursts) - Token consumption: 1 token per command - Quota exceeded: Block until tokens available OR reject with 429

Quota exceeded returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests (RFC 6585). Include Retry-After header with seconds until quota resets. Example: Retry-After: 60 (retry in 60 seconds) Client-friendly error message with quota details (not internal limits).

Hot Tenant Detection identifies sustained quota violations: - Threshold: >80% quota usage for >5 consecutive minutes - Alert: Prometheus alert for hot tenant (potential attack or misconfiguration) - Action: Log warning, emit metric, trigger investigation Do NOT auto-throttle (manual intervention for hot tenants).

Prometheus metrics per tenant (tagged with tenant_id): - tenant_quota_usage_events (Gauge) - Current event rate - tenant_quota_limit_events (Gauge) - Configured limit - tenant_quota_rejected_total (Counter) - Rejected requests count - tenant_quota_percentage (Gauge) - Usage percentage (0-100)

interface.name: TenantQuotaManager Service
interface.description: Central service for per-tenant quota enforcement. Integrates with Bucket4j for rate limiting.

interface.name: Quota Enforcement Interceptor
interface.description: Axon command interceptor for quota enforcement. Rejects commands when quota exceeded.

interface.name: Per-Tenant Quota Configuration
interface.description: Configuration service for per-tenant quota limits. Stored in database for runtime updates.

standards.standard: Constitutional TDD: Integration test validates quota enforcement. Test BEFORE implementing quota manager.

Test rate limiting scenarios: - Below quota: Commands succeed - At quota limit: Commands succeed until tokens exhausted - Above quota: Commands rejected with 429 Too Many Requests - Burst capacity: Short burst allowed (2x refill rate) - Token refill: After waiting, quota resets and commands succeed
Test hot tenant detection: - Sustained high usage (>80% for >5 minutes) → alert triggered - Spike usage (short burst) → no alert (transient) - Metrics validate hot tenant detection logic
Test quota isolation between tenants: - Tenant A exceeds quota → Tenant B unaffected - Concurrent quota enforcement → isolated per tenant - Different quota limits per tenant → enforced correctly

ideas.idea: Test AC1: TenantQuotaManager.checkEventQuota() returns true when below quota
Test AC1: TenantQuotaManager.checkEventQuota() returns false when quota exceeded
Test AC2: Quota limits configurable per tenant (different limits for tenant-a, tenant-b)
Test AC3: Token bucket refills at configured rate (max_events_per_second)
Test AC3: Burst capacity allows short spikes (2x refill rate)
Test AC4: Quota exceeded → 429 Too Many Requests response
Test AC4: Retry-After header includes seconds until quota reset
Test AC5: Prometheus metrics track quota usage per tenant
Test AC5: tenant_quota_rejected_total counter incremented on rejection
Test AC6: Hot tenant detection - sustained >80% usage triggers alert
Test AC6: Hot tenant detection - short spike does NOT trigger alert
Test AC7: Integration test - send 150 commands with 100/sec limit → last 50 rejected
Test multi-tenant isolation: Tenant A quota exceeded, Tenant B continues normally
Test quota reset: Wait for refill period → quota available again
